description: A labeled corpus drawn from one web app's real request fields. The malicious entries are sanitized reproductions of payloads that hit a staging WAF; the benign entries are real, previously-blocked-by-mistake customer inputs that generated support tickets. Feed this through your own ruleset to tune for THIS application's traffic, not a generic corpus.

entries.label: malicious
entries.note: reflected XSS attempt in the ?q= search parameter

entries.label: malicious
entries.note: stored XSS attempt in a product review body

entries.label: benign
entries.text: I love **bold** text and the strong tag.
entries.note: real user bio using allowed markdown/HTML; must not be blocked

entries.label: benign
entries.text: My page onload handler isn't firing in Safari, any ideas?
entries.note: real support message that mentions onload in prose

entries.label: malicious
entries.note: auth bypass attempt on the login form

entries.label: malicious
entries.note: union extraction via the sort= parameter

entries.label: benign
entries.note: real customer name with apostrophe; previously false-blocked

entries.label: benign
entries.text: Order arrived fast; drop me a line if the size runs small.
entries.note: real review using 'drop' as ordinary English

entries.label: malicious
entries.note: url-encoded traversal in the same parameter

entries.label: malicious
entries.note: command injection in a network-diagnostic 'ping host' tool

entries.label: benign
entries.note: a normal IP entered into the same diagnostic tool
